results.rendered_html: 1. tata m. (cf. tāta) chiefly Ved. a father (familiar expression corresponding to nanā, mother),  RV. viii, 91, 5 f. ; ix, 112, 3 ;  AV. ;  TS. iii ;  TBr.  &c. (voc. [like tāta] also term of affection addressed to a son,  AitBr. v, 14, 3 ; vii, 14, 8 ).

results.rendered_html:
1. tata m. Vater (vertrauliche Benennung, entsprechend nanā Mutter)  NIR. 6,6 . kārurahaṃ tato bhiṣagupalaprakṣiṇī nanā  ṚV. 9,112,3 . 8,80,6 .  AV. 5,24,16 .  TS. 3,2,5,5 .  TBR. 1,6,9,7 .  KAUŚ. 88 .  PĀR. GṚHY. 1,5 .  BHĀG. P. 9,4,1 . voc.  AV. 18,4,77 .  AIT. BR. 5,14 . 7,15 .  KAṬHOP. 1,4 .  BHĀG. P. 6,9,40 . 9,4,2 .
— Vgl. tatāmaha, tāta.

results.rendered_html:
2. tata (von 1. tan)
1〉 partic. s. u. tan.
— 2〉 m. Wind  H. an. 2,170 .  MED. t. 21 .
— 3〉 n. parox.  Uṇ. 3,87 . Saiteninstrument  AK. 1,1,7,4 .  TRIK. 3,3,158 .  H. 286 .  H. an.   MED. 
— 4〉 ein best. Metrum (vier Mal ⏑ ⏑ ⏑ ⏑ ⏑ ⏑ – – – – ⏑ –)  COLEBR. Misc. Ess. II, 160  (VII, 8 ).

results.rendered_html:
tata p. p. [tan-kta]
1 Extended, spread;  Śi. 9. 23 , 6. 50 ;  Ki. 5. 11. 
2 Spreading or reaching over, extending to.
3 Covered over, concealed.
4 Protracted, continued.
5 Bent (as a bow).
6 Spreading wide &c.; see tan.
— taḥ Ved.
1 A father.
2 Wind, air.
3 Extent.
4 Offspring, a child (n. also).
5 A son.
— taṃ Any stringed musical instrument.
